plantings.variety: Super Star + Yellow Sweet Spanish (~18 each)

tasks.title: Check for suckers
tasks.description: Remove suckers forming in the crotch between main stem and lateral branches. Indeterminate varieties only (Amish Paste, Comstock, Bonny Best) — leave determinates (Roma, Orange Hat) unpruned. Pinch suckers when small (under 2 inches) for a cleaner break.

tasks.title: Inspect for hornworms — apply Bt if found
tasks.description: Check undersides of leaves on lower third of plant. Look for dark frass (droppings) as first sign. If hornworms found: spray Bacillus thuringiensis (Bt) on affected foliage per label. Hand-pick any large worms already present. Reapply Bt after rain.

tasks.title: Check for suckers
tasks.description: Remove suckers forming in the crotch between main stem and lateral branches. Indeterminate varieties only (Amish Paste, Comstock, Bonny Best) — leave determinates (Roma, Orange Hat) unpruned. Pinch suckers when small (under 2 inches) for a cleaner break.

tasks.title: Pre-fruit feed — bone meal
tasks.description: Side-dress 1 tbsp bone meal per plant once first flowers appear. Supports fruit set and meets phosphorus demand during flowering.

tasks.title: Inspect for early blight
tasks.description: Look for brown spots with yellow halos on lower leaves. Remove affected leaves immediately and dispose (do not compost). Increase airflow by pruning dense interior foliage if severe.

tasks.title: Check for suckers
tasks.description: Remove suckers forming in the crotch between main stem and lateral branches. Indeterminate varieties only (Amish Paste, Comstock, Bonny Best) — leave determinates (Roma, Orange Hat) unpruned. Pinch suckers when small (under 2 inches) for a cleaner break.

tasks.title: Inspect for hornworms — apply Bt if found
tasks.description: Check undersides of leaves on lower third of plant. Look for dark frass (droppings) as first sign. If hornworms found: spray Bacillus thuringiensis (Bt) on affected foliage per label. Hand-pick any large worms already present. Reapply Bt after rain.

tasks.title: Check for suckers
tasks.description: Remove suckers forming in the crotch between main stem and lateral branches. Indeterminate varieties only (Amish Paste, Comstock, Bonny Best) — leave determinates (Roma, Orange Hat) unpruned. Pinch suckers when small (under 2 inches) for a cleaner break.

tasks.title: Inspect for early blight
tasks.description: Look for brown spots with yellow halos on lower leaves. Remove affected leaves immediately and dispose (do not compost). Increase airflow by pruning dense interior foliage if severe.

tasks.title: Mid-season feed — blood meal
tasks.description: Work 1 tbsp blood meal per plant into top inch of soil. Apply once heavy fruiting begins to compensate for nitrogen demand. Skip if foliage is already very dark green — excess nitrogen pushes leaf growth at expense of fruit.

tasks.title: Inspect for hornworms — apply Bt if found
tasks.description: Check undersides of leaves on lower third of plant. Look for dark frass (droppings) as first sign. If hornworms found: spray Bacillus thuringiensis (Bt) on affected foliage per label. Hand-pick any large worms already present. Reapply Bt after rain.

tasks.title: Check for suckers
tasks.description: Remove suckers forming in the crotch between main stem and lateral branches. Indeterminate varieties only (Amish Paste, Comstock, Bonny Best) — leave determinates (Roma, Orange Hat) unpruned. Pinch suckers when small (under 2 inches) for a cleaner break.

tasks.description: Remove suckers forming in the crotch between main stem and lateral branches. Indeterminate varieties only (Amish Paste, Comstock, Bonny Best) — leave determinates (Roma, Orange Hat) unpruned. Pinch suckers when small (under 2 inches) for a cleaner break.

tasks.description: Check undersides of leaves on lower third of plant. Look for dark frass (droppings) as first sign. If hornworms found: spray Bacillus thuringiensis (Bt) on affected foliage per label. Hand-pick any large worms already present. Reapply Bt after rain.

tasks.title: Inspect for early blight
tasks.description: Look for brown spots with yellow halos on lower leaves. Remove affected leaves immediately and dispose (do not compost). Increase airflow by pruning dense interior foliage if severe.

tasks.title: First harvest check
tasks.description: Roma and paste types: firm and fully colored. Slicers: slight give when squeezed. Do not wait for perfect color in heat — fully vine-ripened tomatoes go soft fast in summer temperatures.

tasks.title: Check for suckers
tasks.description: Remove suckers forming in the crotch between main stem and lateral branches. Indeterminate varieties only (Amish Paste, Comstock, Bonny Best) — leave determinates (Roma, Orange Hat) unpruned. Pinch suckers when small (under 2 inches) for a cleaner break.

tasks.description: Check undersides of leaves on lower third of plant. Look for dark frass (droppings) as first sign. If hornworms found: spray Bacillus thuringiensis (Bt) on affected foliage per label. Hand-pick any large worms already present. Reapply Bt after rain.

tasks.title: Check for suckers
tasks.description: Remove suckers forming in the crotch between main stem and lateral branches. Indeterminate varieties only (Amish Paste, Comstock, Bonny Best) — leave determinates (Roma, Orange Hat) unpruned. Pinch suckers when small (under 2 inches) for a cleaner break.

tasks.description: Look for brown spots with yellow halos on lower leaves. Remove affected leaves immediately and dispose (do not compost). Increase airflow by pruning dense interior foliage if severe.

tasks.title: Inspect for hornworms — apply Bt if found
tasks.description: Check undersides of leaves on lower third of plant. Look for dark frass (droppings) as first sign. If hornworms found: spray Bacillus thuringiensis (Bt) on affected foliage per label. Hand-pick any large worms already present. Reapply Bt after rain.

tasks.title: Finish hilling the potato bags
tasks.description: June 14 log shows two bags at 2/3 hilled and three at 1/3 — complete coverage now before plant growth outpaces stem burial in the heat.

tasks.title: Fall renovation
tasks.description: After last harvest in fall, cut all foliage back to 1 inch above crown. Remove old mulch and debris. Add 1 inch of fresh compost to the bed. Apply fall Berry-Tone feeding at label rate. This resets the plant for next year.

tasks.title: Early spring feed — Berry-Tone
tasks.description: Apply True Organic Berry-Tone at label rate (approximately 1/2 cup per 5 sq ft of bed) as soon as new growth appears in early spring. Work lightly into top inch of soil and water in well.

logs.note: Bajillion flowers
Bajillion flowers
Bajillion flowers
Watering hard for one hour. Also added more nutrients in the form of berry food and worm castings to try and help.
Final yield only 1 lb. Delicious. I think I needed to plant a little earlier and water more generously.
Looking really rough. Added fertilizer and watered full hose for 1 hour to try and kinda bring it back but she is a sad little tree this year.
4 sprouted. 2 knocked back by a late frost but I think they will come back
Sprouts! Sprouted today already a couple inches tall. Six of the 8 so far.

payload.headline: 91°F today, 98°F Sunday — run zones Saturday morning.
weather_notes: Run both zones early Saturday morning — zones last ran July 2 and Independence Day's 95°F hits two days dry; water before holiday activities close the window.
Do all bed work tonight or before 8 AM Saturday — 95°F Independence Day and 98°F Sunday with zero rain close every afternoon outdoor window through the holiday weekend.
Check e1-e4 transplant soil tonight — 13-day plants begin their first stretch of back-to-back 91-98°F days and any dry rootball pockets need hand-watering before Saturday.

suggested_tasks.title: Feed c2-c4 bush beans Saturday morning
suggested_tasks.description: 36-day bush beans have no logged fertilizer — apply fish emulsion Saturday morning before Sunday's 98°F closes the comfortable feeding window.

payload.headline: Heat spikes to 98°F Sunday — run zones again by Friday.
weather_notes: Run both zones again by Friday morning — 95°F Saturday and 98°F Sunday exceed what today's irrigation alone will cover.
Do all bed work before 9 AM Friday through Sunday — 91-98°F heat with zero rain forecast closes all afternoon outdoor windows for the holiday weekend.
Top up mulch on e1-e4 before Friday — 12-day transplants need 2-3 inches of cover to buffer the 95-98°F weekend heat spike.

surfaced_tasks.title: Finish hilling the potato bags
surfaced_tasks.reason: 7 days overdue — Jun 24 log confirms 3 bags need one final hill; Thursday morning is the last cool window before 91-98°F closes outdoor work through the weekend.

suggested_tasks.title: Check peach tree for signs of life
suggested_tasks.description: 29 days since the Jun 3 'possibly dead' log with no follow-up — assess before Sunday's 98°F to decide on intervention or replacement.

payload.headline: Heat back to 93°F by Friday — run zones and feed beans.
weather_notes: Run both Rachio zones this morning — 3 days dry with heat climbing to 91–93°F by July 3–4 and zero rain in the forecast.
Mulch e1–e4 tomato beds before Friday — 11-day transplants need 2–3 inches of mulch to hold soil moisture through the 93°F weekend.
Do all bed work before 9 AM Wednesday through Sunday — 91–93°F with no rain relief through Independence Day.

suggested_tasks.title: Side-dress c2–c4 beans with fish emulsion
suggested_tasks.description: 34 days from seeding with no fertilizer logged — apply fish emulsion before Sunday's 93°F to support the push to first harvest.

payload.headline: Zone 2 needs water before Wednesday's heat returns.
weather_notes: Run zone 2 today for w1, w4, and blackberry — last watered Jun 26 and this week's light drizzle never reached 0.1in.
Keep w2 and w3 garlic off today's zone 2 run — dry-down continues toward the mid-July harvest.
Schedule zone 1's next run for Thursday — last watered Jun 28, and Thursday's 90°F will make it 4 days dry by then.

surfaced_tasks.title: Side-dress c1 beans with fish emulsion
surfaced_tasks.reason: Due tomorrow at 58 days since seeding — today's mild 81°F is the last cool day before heat climbs to 87-90°F Wed-Fri.

payload.headline: Cool break — finish potato hilling before heat returns.
weather_notes: Skip all irrigation through Monday — both zones ran this morning and tonight's 30% rain chance extends the moisture window.
Exclude w2 and w3 garlic from zone 2's next run — 17 days to harvest and today's watering should be their last before cure.
Do outdoor tasks today or Monday — 68°F today and 73°F Monday are the only cool windows before heat climbs back to 80°F Tuesday.

surfaced_tasks.title: Finish hilling the potato bags
surfaced_tasks.reason: 3 days overdue — Jun 24 log confirms 3 bags still need one final hill; today's 68°F break is the first practical outdoor window after a week of 95–99°F heat.

suggested_tasks.title: Side-dress c1 beans with fish emulsion
suggested_tasks.description: 56 days from seeding with no logged fertilizer application — feed now before pod set begins, using this week's cool 68–80°F window.

payload.headline: 17 days to garlic harvest — check for heat-advanced browning.
weather_notes: Skip all irrigation through Monday — zones ran this morning and Sunday-night storm chances will maintain soil moisture into a clear Monday.
Resume zone 1 and zone 2 by Tuesday morning — 80°F arrives dry with no rain in the 7-day outlook; first scheduled run after the weekend gap.
Check w2 and w3 garlic leaf browning before Tuesday's warmup — last week's 95–99°F peak may have pulled the July 15 harvest window forward by 1-2 weeks.

suggested_tasks.title: Inspect c1 beans: support and pod set
suggested_tasks.description: Good Mother Stollard is a pole bean at 56 days — now in early pod set with no log since May 13; confirm trellis support is in place before vines sprawl further.

payload.headline: Heat breaks — hill potato bags this morning before storms.
weather_notes: Do all outdoor work before 9 AM today — Red Flag Warning active through midnight with gusty winds and extreme low humidity.
Skip both zone runs through Sunday — zone 2 ran yesterday and 50% storm chance today plus 41% Sunday cover the moisture gap.
Reassess irrigation Monday — check soil after weekend storms clear before scheduling the next zone run.

surfaced_tasks.title: Finish hilling the potato bags
surfaced_tasks.reason: 2 days overdue — Jun 24 log confirms 3 bags still need one final hill; today's 78°F break is the first practical outdoor window after a week of 95-99°F heat.

surfaced_tasks.title: Harvest check
surfaced_tasks.reason: Five consecutive days of 95-99°F heat can accelerate garlic maturation by a week — check leaf browning on w2 now rather than waiting for the Jul 5 due date to avoid missing the harvest window.

surfaced_tasks.title: Harvest check
surfaced_tasks.reason: Same heat-acceleration risk as w2 — check Italian Red leaf browning alongside w2 this weekend to confirm whether the Jul 15 harvest window has shifted earlier.

payload.headline: Finish potatoes, pick blackberries — 96°F Red Flag today.
weather_notes: Do all garden work before 9 AM — Red Flag Warning active through Saturday night with 96°F and 36 mph gusts makes afternoon conditions dangerous.
Skip zone runs today — both zones ran yesterday and 96°F meets the 1-day no-water threshold.
Hold Saturday morning irrigation — 30% storm chance Saturday afternoon may cover beds before zones need to run.
Skip Sunday zone irrigation — 50% rain chance should handle the moisture gap; reassess schedule Monday based on actual rainfall.

surfaced_tasks.title: Finish hilling the potato bags
surfaced_tasks.reason: 1 day overdue — Jun 24 log confirms 3 bags still need one final hill; do it this morning before 96°F heat and weekend rain close the window.

suggested_tasks.title: Pick ripe blackberries before weekend rain
suggested_tasks.description: Triple Crown berries have been ripening 5 days in 95-98°F heat — pick today before Saturday storms soften ripe fruit on the cane.

payload.headline: Stake new tomatoes today — 36 mph gusts hit Friday.
weather_notes: Do all garden work before 9 AM Friday — Red Flag Warning through June 28 with 36 mph gusts and extreme low humidity.
Skip zone runs Friday — zones ran today and tomorrow's 95°F is within the 1-day watering threshold; no irrigation needed.
Hold Saturday irrigation until afternoon — 50% rain chance may cover soil moisture needs before zones need to run.
Keep w2 and w3 garlic off all irrigation — 20 days to expected July 15 harvest and dry-down must hold.

surfaced_tasks.title: Finish hilling the potato bags
surfaced_tasks.reason: Due today — yesterday's log confirms 3 bags still need one final hill to reach full coverage.

suggested_tasks.title: Stake e1-e4 transplants before Friday gusts
suggested_tasks.description: 5-day-old transplants face 36 mph gusts Friday — stake now before wind rocks rootballs loose before they establish.

payload.headline: Stake e1-e4 now — Friday brings 35 mph wind gusts.
weather_notes: Exclude w2 and w3 garlic from zone 2's next run — harvest is 21 days out and continued irrigation now deteriorates wrapper quality.
Check soil before running zones Thursday — tonight's 50% storm chance may deliver enough moisture to push the next irrigation window to Friday.
Do all garden work before 9 AM through Friday — Fire Weather Watch active through June 28 with extreme low humidity and gusting winds.

surfaced_tasks.title: Finish hilling the potato bags
surfaced_tasks.reason: Due tomorrow; bags were partially hilled June 14 and Friday's 35 mph gusts close the practical outdoor work window — complete today or early Thursday morning.

suggested_tasks.title: Stake e1-e4 tomatoes before Friday gusts
suggested_tasks.description: 4-day-old transplants face 35 mph south wind gusts Friday — stake now before root-rocking loosens rootballs before establishment.

payload.headline: New tomatoes day 3 in 96°F heat — run zones now.
weather_notes: Run zone 1 this morning — 3-day-old e1-e4 transplants last watered June 21 cannot go 3 days dry with 96°F today and 97°F Wednesday.
Run zone 2 this morning for w1, w4, and blackberry — last watered June 21; skip w2 and w3 garlic.
Hold Wednesday's zone runs if afternoon storms deliver — 20% storm chance that afternoon; check soil before scheduling the next run.

suggested_tasks.title: Hand-water e1-e4 transplants at stem base daily
suggested_tasks.description: 3-day-old transplants in 96-97°F heat need daily soil checks beyond the zone schedule — hand-water at the base on any day zone 1 doesn't run.

suggested_tasks.title: Finish hilling the potato bags
suggested_tasks.description: June 14 log shows two bags at 2/3 hilled and three at 1/3 — complete coverage now before plant growth outpaces stem burial in the heat.

payload.headline: Garlic off zone 2 now — 24 days to harvest.
weather_notes: Do all garden work before 9 AM Monday through Wednesday — heat climbs from 92°F to 97°F with no rain in the 7-day forecast.
Re-run zone 1 by Wednesday morning — today's run will be 3 days old at Wednesday's 97°F peak, which may stress 24-day bean seedlings in c2–c4 and the potato bags.

surfaced_tasks.title: Stop watering
surfaced_tasks.reason: Due today — zone 2 ran this morning but w2 and w3 must be excluded from all future zone 2 runs now; both garlic beds are 24 days from harvest and the dry-down cannot wait another day.

suggested_tasks.title: Harvest ripe blackberries before heat peaks
suggested_tasks.description: Berries first observed June 14 — 7 days on the vine plus 95–97°F heat arriving Tuesday will over-ripen and soften remaining fruit within 1–2 days.

suggested_tasks.title: Check e1 Amish Paste for suckers
suggested_tasks.description: At 43 days with no active sucker task for this planting, indeterminate Amish Paste is in peak lateral growth — 97°F heat by Wednesday will push suckers past the 2-inch pinch window.

payload.headline: Garlic cutoff today — 97°F heat wave arrives mid-week.
weather_notes: Inspect e1 tomatoes for hornworms and blight before 9 AM Mon–Wed — 92°F Monday escalates to 97°F Wednesday, peaking both pest feeding and disease risk.
Check potato_bags moisture daily this week — grow bags dry significantly faster than ground beds during the 95-97°F mid-week stretch with zero rain forecast.
Mulch exposed soil on c2–c4 bean beds before Tuesday — 24-day-old seedlings face their first 95°F day with no rain in the forecast.

surfaced_tasks.title: Stop watering
surfaced_tasks.reason: Due today — zone 2 ran this morning on the deadline; remove w2 from all future zone 2 cycles with harvest 24 days out.

surfaced_tasks.title: Inspect for hornworms — apply Bt if found
surfaced_tasks.reason: Overdue by 1 day on 43-day Amish Paste — 95-97°F mid-week heat drives peak hornworm feeding; check lower leaves for frass this morning.

suggested_tasks.title: Harvest ripe blackberries before heat spoils them
suggested_tasks.description: Berries first observed Jun 14 — 7 days of 89-97°F heat means fruit is at or past peak and will soften fast in this week's 95°F+ stretch.

payload.headline: Stop w2 garlic water now — deadline is tomorrow.
weather_notes: Complete all garden tasks before 10 AM — Fire Weather Watch active today until 10 PM MDT brings gusty wind and extreme low humidity.
Check potato_bags soil moisture today — grow bags dry out faster than ground beds under Fire Weather Watch conditions and bags last watered June 18.
Run zone 1 Sunday morning if tonight's isolated storms (30% chance) deliver nothing — tomatoes and beans last watered June 18 face 88–91°F heat through Monday.

surfaced_tasks.title: Stop watering
surfaced_tasks.reason: Due tomorrow — zone 2 last ran June 18 and harvest is 25 days out; exclude w2 from all future zone 2 runs starting today.

surfaced_tasks.title: Inspect for hornworms — apply Bt if found
surfaced_tasks.reason: Due today on 42-day Amish Paste — 7 days of 89–97°F heat drives peak hornworm feeding; check lower leaves for frass this morning.

suggested_tasks.title: Harvest ripe blackberries before they soften
suggested_tasks.description: Berries first observed June 14 — 6 days of 89–97°F heat will have ripened and begun softening fruit; pick now before they pass peak.
